sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The SPACE action didn't seem to do much - the board changed but the level didn't complete. Let me check what actually changed. Looking at the images, they all look the same. Let me check the segmentation to see if anything changed.
Let me reconsider the game. I have three F-shaped objects:
1. Charcoal F (dark gray) on the left
2. Black F in the middle
3. Yellow F on the right
The black F has been moved left to be adjacent to the charcoal F. SPACE didn't complete the level.
Maybe the goal is to overlap all three F shapes? Or maybe I need to move the black F to overlap with the yellow F instead? Or perhaps I need to move the yellow F to overlap with the others?
Let me check what changed after the SPACE action by comparing frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("BEFORE:")
for n in before:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        maxr = max(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        maxc = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"  color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} id={n['id']} bbox=[{minr},{minc}]-[{maxr},{maxc}]")
print("AFTER:")
for n in after:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        maxr = max(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        maxc = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"  color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} id={n['id']} bbox=[{minr},{minc}]-[{maxr},{maxc}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The only change after SPACE was in the right-side bar (the timer/progress bar): the black portion (id=3) grew from 31 to 32 pixels and the yellow portion (id=4) shrank from 33 to 32 pixels. The actual game objects (F shapes) didn't change at all.
So SPACE just advances the timer bar. The F shapes are unchanged. I need to rethink the goal.
Looking at the three F shapes:
- Charcoal F: bbox=[45,24]-[53,29] 
- Black F: bbox=[45,30]-[53,38]
- Yellow F: bbox=[45,51]-[53,59]
The black F is now adjacent to the charcoal F. Maybe I need to move the black F to overlap with the yellow F instead? Or maybe I need to move all three to overlap?
Wait, let me reconsider. The black F was originally at cols 51-59 (overlapping the yellow F). I moved it LEFT 7 times to bring it to cols 30-38. But maybe the goal was to overlap the black F with the yellow F, not the charcoal F?
Let me try moving the black F RIGHT to overlap with the yellow F. But first, let me understand what controls the black F. It seems like LEFT/RIGHT moves the black F. Let me try moving it right to overlap with the yellow F.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The black F was moved LEFT away from the yellow F. Maybe the goal is to overlap black F with yellow F.
# Move RIGHT to bring black F toward yellow F (which is at cols 51-59)
# Black F is at cols 30-38, need to move to overlap with 51-59
# That's about 13 steps right
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
